# Csv Edit

## Overview

csv_edit ‚Äî in-place and copy editing of stored CSV files.

## Tool

| Tool | Summary | Permissions | Requires approval | Core |
| --- | --- | --- | :---: | :---: |
| `csv_edit` | Edit a stored CSV file: add/update/delete columns and rows, sort, deduplicate, cast types, split columns, and fill nulls. Returns a new file_id (or the same one when editing an _edited.csv file). | `core:csv_edit` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |

## Environment variables

The variables below are derived automatically from each tool's `config_schema`. Set them in your `.env` (or via your secrets backend) to seed defaults for every organisation. **Per-org DB config takes precedence over env vars.**

_No configuration-derived environment variables._

## Operations

### `csv_edit`

- **`add_column`** ‚Äî append a new column.

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `column` | `string` | ‚Äî | Target column name. - add_column: name of the new column to create. - set_column / fill_nulls: name of the existing column to update. - cast_column / split_column: column to operate on. |
  | `value` | `object` | ‚Äî | Value source spec for add_column, set_column, fill_nulls. Supported types:   {"type": "literal", "value": <any>}   {"type": "column", "column": "X"}   {"type": "concat", "parts": [{"column":"A"},{"literal":" - "},{"column":"B"}], "sep": ""}   {"type": "arith", "expr": "A + B * 2"} |
  | `overwrite` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | add_column only. When true, overwrite if the column already exists. Default false. |

- **`set_column`** ‚Äî update values in an existing column (optionally filtered).

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `column` | `string` | ‚Äî | Target column name. - add_column: name of the new column to create. - set_column / fill_nulls: name of the existing column to update. - cast_column / split_column: column to operate on. |
  | `value` | `object` | ‚Äî | Value source spec for add_column, set_column, fill_nulls. Supported types:   {"type": "literal", "value": <any>}   {"type": "column", "column": "X"}   {"type": "concat", "parts": [{"column":"A"},{"literal":" - "},{"column":"B"}], "sep": ""}   {"type": "arith", "expr": "A + B * 2"} |

- **`delete_rows`** ‚Äî remove rows matching a filter.

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `file_id` | `string` | ‚úì | UUID of the source CSV file (GSageFile.id). |
  | `column` | `string` | ‚Äî | Target column name. - add_column: name of the new column to create. - set_column / fill_nulls: name of the existing column to update. - cast_column / split_column: column to operate on. |
  | `value` | `object` | ‚Äî | Value source spec for add_column, set_column, fill_nulls. Supported types:   {"type": "literal", "value": <any>}   {"type": "column", "column": "X"}   {"type": "concat", "parts": [{"column":"A"},{"literal":" - "},{"column":"B"}], "sep": ""}   {"type": "arith", "expr": "A + B * 2"} |
  | `overwrite` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | add_column only. When true, overwrite if the column already exists. Default false. |
  | `columns` | `array` | ‚Äî | delete_columns: list of column names to drop. |
  | `column_map` | `object` | ‚Äî | rename_columns: mapping of {old_name: new_name}. |
  | `by` | `array` | ‚Äî | sort: list of sort specs. |
  | `subset` | `array` | ‚Äî | deduplicate: columns to consider for duplicates. Omit to use all columns. |
  | `keep` | `string` | ‚Äî | deduplicate: which duplicate to keep. 'none' removes all rows that have any duplicate. Default 'first'. |
  | `target_type` | `string` | ‚Äî | cast_column: target data type. |
  | `date_format` | `string` | ‚Äî | cast_column: strftime format string for date/datetime parsing (e.g. '%Y-%m-%d', '%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S'). Omit to let Polars auto-detect. |
  | `strict` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | cast_column: when false (default), unparseable values become null instead of raising an error. |
  | `separator` | `string` | ‚Äî | split_column: literal separator string (e.g. ':'). |
  | `pattern` | `string` | ‚Äî | split_column: regex pattern to split on (overrides separator). |
  | `new_columns` | `array` | ‚Äî | split_column: names for the resulting columns. Length must equal 'n'. Auto-named if omitted. |
  | `n` | `integer` | ‚Äî | split_column: number of parts to split into. |
  | `drop_original` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | split_column: remove the source column. Default false. |
  | `filter` | `object` | ‚Äî | Structured row filter. Mutually exclusive with 'where_sql'. Use exactly one of 'all' (AND) or 'any' (OR):   {"all": [{"column":"A","op":"contains","value":"foo","ignore_case":true}]} Supported ops: eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, contains, starts_with, ends_with, regex, in, not_in, is_null, is_not_null. |
  | `where_sql` | `string` | ‚Äî | DuckDB WHERE-clause fragment (advanced). Mutually exclusive with 'filter'. Example: "status = 'active' AND score > 80". The CSV is exposed as a view named 'csv'. DDL / DML / filesystem functions are rejected. |
  | `delimiter` | `string` | ‚Äî | Override delimiter detection. Allowed: ',', ';', '\t', '\|'. Omit for auto-detect. |
  | `encoding` | `string` | ‚Äî | Override encoding detection (e.g. 'utf-8', 'latin-1'). |
  | `create_new` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | Controls whether the result is saved as a new file or overwrites the source in-place. - true: always create a new file with an incremented suffix ('_edited.csv', '_edited2.csv', '_edited3.csv', ‚Ä¶). The system checks existing filenames and picks the first available one. - false (default for all actions except filter_to_new): overwrite the source file in-place when its name already ends with '_edited*.csv'. If the source is an original file (no '_edited*' suffix), a new '_edited.csv' is always created regardless ‚Äî the original is never overwritten. - filter_to_new default: true (materialises a subset into a new file). |

- **`delete_columns`** ‚Äî drop one or more columns.

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `columns` | `array` | ‚Äî | delete_columns: list of column names to drop. |

- **`rename_columns`** ‚Äî rename columns via an old‚Üínew mapping.

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `column_map` | `object` | ‚Äî | rename_columns: mapping of {old_name: new_name}. |

- **`sort`** ‚Äî sort rows by one or more columns.

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `by` | `array` | ‚Äî | sort: list of sort specs. |

- **`deduplicate`** ‚Äî drop duplicate rows.

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `subset` | `array` | ‚Äî | deduplicate: columns to consider for duplicates. Omit to use all columns. |
  | `keep` | `string` | ‚Äî | deduplicate: which duplicate to keep. 'none' removes all rows that have any duplicate. Default 'first'. |

- **`filter_to_new`** ‚Äî keep only rows matching a filter.

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `create_new` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | Controls whether the result is saved as a new file or overwrites the source in-place. - true: always create a new file with an incremented suffix ('_edited.csv', '_edited2.csv', '_edited3.csv', ‚Ä¶). The system checks existing filenames and picks the first available one. - false (default for all actions except filter_to_new): overwrite the source file in-place when its name already ends with '_edited*.csv'. If the source is an original file (no '_edited*' suffix), a new '_edited.csv' is always created regardless ‚Äî the original is never overwritten. - filter_to_new default: true (materialises a subset into a new file). |

- **`cast_column`** ‚Äî change a column's data type.

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `column` | `string` | ‚Äî | Target column name. - add_column: name of the new column to create. - set_column / fill_nulls: name of the existing column to update. - cast_column / split_column: column to operate on. |
  | `target_type` | `string` | ‚Äî | cast_column: target data type. |
  | `date_format` | `string` | ‚Äî | cast_column: strftime format string for date/datetime parsing (e.g. '%Y-%m-%d', '%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S'). Omit to let Polars auto-detect. |
  | `strict` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | cast_column: when false (default), unparseable values become null instead of raising an error. |

- **`split_column`** ‚Äî split one column into N columns.

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `column` | `string` | ‚Äî | Target column name. - add_column: name of the new column to create. - set_column / fill_nulls: name of the existing column to update. - cast_column / split_column: column to operate on. |
  | `separator` | `string` | ‚Äî | split_column: literal separator string (e.g. ':'). |
  | `pattern` | `string` | ‚Äî | split_column: regex pattern to split on (overrides separator). |
  | `new_columns` | `array` | ‚Äî | split_column: names for the resulting columns. Length must equal 'n'. Auto-named if omitted. |
  | `n` | `integer` | ‚Äî | split_column: number of parts to split into. |
  | `drop_original` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | split_column: remove the source column. Default false. |

- **`fill_nulls`** ‚Äî fill null values with a literal or another column.

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `column` | `string` | ‚Äî | Target column name. - add_column: name of the new column to create. - set_column / fill_nulls: name of the existing column to update. - cast_column / split_column: column to operate on. |
  | `value` | `object` | ‚Äî | Value source spec for add_column, set_column, fill_nulls. Supported types:   {"type": "literal", "value": <any>}   {"type": "column", "column": "X"}   {"type": "concat", "parts": [{"column":"A"},{"literal":" - "},{"column":"B"}], "sep": ""}   {"type": "arith", "expr": "A + B * 2"} |
